title: About
text:
I’m a statistician based in Boston with a PhD in Statistics from Boston University and a background in statistical consulting and graduate research. I’m interested in the foundations of statistical inference and what it actually means to draw valid conclusions from data.
I think a lot about epistemology — not in an abstract philosophical way, but in a practical sense: how do we know what we know, and how often are we fooling ourselves?
This site is where I write about statistics and whatever else I’m thinking about.

title: Russell Goebel
text:
I’m a statistician with a background in consulting and academic research. I care about epistemology, rigor, and the gap between how statistics is taught and how it’s actually practiced.
This site is where I think out loud — about statistical methods, data analysis, and occasionally other things.
About me | Writing
